title: chrysobalanus icaco subsp. orbicularis
intro: Chrysobalanus icaco subsp. orbicularis, also known as the West Indian jicama or craboo, is a subspecies of tree native to the West Indies and Central America, belonging to the family Chrysobalanaceae. The roots and bark of the plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to their high tannin and alkaloid content. The plant's medicinal properties have been associated with its potential health benefits, such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of Chrysobalanus icaco subsp. orbicularis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support immune function, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
